Victor Jiao
Vol. III San Francisco Apr 2026
Projects Art About Résumé

About

Three Volumes · The Longer Version

Vol. I · New York · 1991–2013 (ish)

Queens, Stuyvesant, and finding a shape

Vol. I opens in Queens. I went to Stuyvesant — the kind of school where "competitive math" and "leading hackathons" are things people actually do at fifteen — and spent a lot of it singing in chorus, playing in the band, and fencing.

I found the shape of the person I'd become in that mix: someone who liked being specific, liked being part of groups, and liked the feeling of getting really good at something you could share with other people.

This is a rough draft based on what I know — rewrite in your voice. Dates, specific activities, and the framing are yours to sharpen.

Vol. II · Chicago + Early Career · 2013–2020 (ish)

UChicago, a cappella, and getting into graphics

Vol. II opens at the University of Chicago, where I did a bachelor's and a master's in computer science. Sang with Voices in Your Head, the a cappella group that went to the ICCA Finals three times while I was there. Did ICPC. Took the advanced coursework — systems, ML, distributed systems (still listed on my LinkedIn somewhere). Interned widely.

Graduated out into Magic Leap, working on graphics systems. Then Google Stadia, on developer tooling and game-performance measurement — I was pretty aggressive about frame rate there and it stuck as a thing I care about. By the end of this volume I'd moved out west.

Rough draft. Internship list, specific coursework, project moments at Magic Leap / Stadia — all yours to fill in. Linked to LinkedIn assuming the detail lives there.

Vol. III · San Francisco · 2020 (ish) – present

SF, community, and Meta Reality Labs

Vol. III opens with a move to San Francisco, just as the pandemic was starting to lift. Senior engineer at Meta Reality Labs now, working on interaction systems.

Earlier in this volume: co-founded The Chromeatics, Google SFO's a cappella group; re-started Pride@SFO for a stretch; played with NAVGA for a bit.

These days I sing with the San Francisco Gay Men's Choir, throw pottery and do a bit of woodworking, and host AI hack nights at home. The throughline — as far as I can tell from inside it — is that I'm spending more time now on what seems to matter most: finding and building community, and trying to grow as a human.

If you want to reach out, email is best. The résumé-shaped version is here.

Set in Fraunces & JetBrains Mono Hand-written CSS · OKLCH palette
